hero.subtitle: The most comprehensive guide to living, working, and settling down in Uruguay—created by people who chose to stay.
hero.event_fallback: First Thursday of every month · Montevideo
hero.join_mini_desc: Join the community and get the welcome guide sent to your inbox.

categories.title: Everything you need, all in one place.
categories.desc: Sixteen sections with in-depth guides on every aspect of living in Uruguay.

items.title: Getting Started and Arrival
items.desc: The First 48 Hours — SIM card, currency exchange, bank, essential apps.

items.title: Visas and Legal Matters
items.desc: Tourist visa, digital nomad permit, residency — step by step, in plain language.

items.title: Neighborhoods
items.desc: Pocitos, Palermo, Old Town — find your perfect spot in Montevideo.

items.title: Housing and Rentals
items.desc: How to find an apartment, lease agreements in Spanish, up-to-date prices.

items.title: Work and coworking
items.desc: The best coworking spaces and cafés where you can actually get some work done.

items.title: Cost of living
items.desc: Actual budgets, USD vs. peso strategy, tax advantages.

items.title: Health and Wellness
items.desc: Health insurance providers, international insurance, pharmacies, and recommended doctors.

items.title: Transportation
items.desc: STM, Uber, intercity travel, how to get to Punta del Este.

items.title: Food and Restaurants
items.desc: From Goat to Hidden Grills — The Ultimate Food Guide

items.title: Culture and language
items.desc: Mate, Carnival, tango, the River Plate region, and how to fit in with the locals.

items.title: Parks and Nature
items.desc: La Rambla, Parque Rodó, and nature getaways from the city.

items.title: Hidden gems
items.desc: Cabo Polonio, Punta del Diablo, Salto — what tourists don´t see.

directory.label: Where to live
directory.title: Not sure where to stay in Uruguay?
directory.desc: Answer a few questions about your lifestyle and budget. We´ll show you the places that best suit you—from Montevideo to Cabo Polonio.

explore.title: Discover Uruguay
explore.subtitle: Travel itineraries by interest and duration — from weekend getaways to in-depth experiences.

items.title: Surfing & Coast
items.desc: Punta del Este, La Pedrera, Cabo Polonio, and Punta del Diablo.

items.title: History & Heritage
items.desc: Colonia del Sacramento, Fray Bentos, and the secrets of the western coast.

items.title: Northern Hot Springs
items.desc: Salto, Paysandú, and the region’s most accessible natural hot springs.

items.title: Wildlife & Nature
items.desc: Cabo Polonio, sea lions, penguins, and the starriest sky.

resources.title: Resources for planning your life in Uruguay
resources.desc: Cost of living calculator and links to the best external resources—all in one place.

testimonials.title: From people like you
testimonials.q1: I moved to Montevideo from Berlin with no plan. This site was the first thing I bookmarked. The neighborhoods guide alone saved me weeks of confusion.
testimonials.q2: The WhatsApp group for newcomers was a lifesaver. Someone from the community helped me find an apartment in 3 days.
testimonials.q3: Finally a guide that doesn't treat Uruguay as a stopover. The visa section is the clearest explanation I found anywhere online.

pdf_banner.title: Download the free guide
pdf_banner.desc: "Your First 30 Days in Uruguay" — everything you need to know before and after you arrive, in a PDF you can take with you.
pdf_banner.note: No spam. Just the guide. Promise.
